channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump provides details behind the dramatic rescue of an American airman trapped in Iran
item.description: President Trump revealed many of the dramatic details on how the U.S. military scrambled to rescue two members of fighter jet that was shot down deep inside Iran.
item.content:encoded: President Trump revealed many of the dramatic details on how the U.S. military scrambled to rescue two members of fighter jet that was shot down deep inside Iran.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Trump reiterates threats to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges
item.description: The president has had mixed messages about how and when the U.S.-Israel-led war in Iran will end.
item.content:encoded:
The president has had mixed messages about how and when the U.S.-Israel-led war in Iran will end.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants, bridges. And, Artemis II readies for lunar flyby
item.description: Trump threatened to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz. And, NASA's Artemis II crew prepares to make its closest approach to the moon.
item.content:encoded:
Trump threatened to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz. And, NASA's Artemis II crew prepares to make its closest approach to the moon.

(Image credit: Pool)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: Iran rejects a U.S. ceasefire plan as Trump again threatens to bomb its infrastructure
item.description: Iran's top officials pushed back against a U.S. ceasefire plan and President Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, striking a defiant tone as the warring sides traded missile attacks.
item.content:encoded:
Iran's top officials pushed back against a U.S. ceasefire plan and President Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, striking a defiant tone as the warring sides traded missile attacks.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: A music festival booked Kanye West, now known as Ye, and lost major sponsors
item.description: The rapper Ye was announced as the headliner for the Wireless Festival in London. He's gained notoriety over the years for his antisemitic comments and activities glorifying Nazis.
item.content:encoded:
The rapper Ye was announced as the headliner for the Wireless Festival in London. He's gained notoriety over the years for his antisemitic comments and activities glorifying Nazis.

(Image credit: Hector Retamal)
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby

item.title: Will Hungary's far-right leader Viktor Orban be voted out of power?
item.description: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Zsuzsanna Vegh, program officer at the German Marshall Fund of the United States, about what's at stake in the upcoming election in Hungary.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Zsuzsanna Vegh, program officer at the German Marshall Fund of the United States, about what's at stake in the upcoming election in Hungary.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: US airman rescued after his fighter jet was shot down in Iran
item.description: NPR's Tom Bowman reports on the race to rescue a U.S. airman after his fighter jet was shot down.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Tom Bowman reports on the race to rescue a U.S. airman after his fighter jet was shot down.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman

item.title: In Lebanon, more than 50 medics have been killed by Israel. Some say they're targeted
item.description: Lebanon says at least 54 health workers are among more than 1,400 people killed by Israel during the current invasion. Human rights groups say first responders are being targeted — something Israel denies.
item.content:encoded:
Lebanon says at least 54 health workers are among more than 1,400 people killed by Israel during the current invasion. Human rights groups say first responders are being targeted — something Israel denies.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Trump unleashes curse-filled social media rant at Iran after U.S. rescues colonel
item.description: In a profanity-laden post on Truth Social, President Trump lashed out at Iran and injected new volatility into the conflict, hours after U.S. forces carried out a high-risk rescue mission.
item.content:encoded:
In a profanity-laden post on Truth Social, President Trump lashed out at Iran and injected new volatility into the conflict, hours after U.S. forces carried out a high-risk rescue mission.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Local culture shapes the Zairean liturgy
item.description: Easter in the Democratic Republic of the Congo—where congregants celebrate with the Zairean Rite, the only Vatican-approved liturgy shaped by local culture, alive with song, dance, and striking ritual.
item.content:encoded: Easter in the Democratic Republic of the Congo—where congregants celebrate with the Zairean Rite, the only Vatican-approved liturgy shaped by local culture, alive with song, dance, and striking ritual.
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone
